data.title: Coinbase has listed OPINION (OPN).
data.description: Coinbase announced on its X platform that OPINION (OPN) is now available on its website and app, allowing users to buy, sell, convert, send, receive, or store OPN assets.

data.title: CleanSpark: February mining yielded 568 BTC, bringing the total holdings to 13,363 BTC.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company CleanSpark released its latest Bitcoin mining and operations update report, disclosing that it mined 568 BTC in February, bringing its year-to-date output to 1,141 BTC. The company also disclosed that it sold 553.02 BTC last month at a price of $66,279, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 13,363 BTC. (PRNewswire)

data.title: European Nations Extend Support to Gulf Amid Ongoing Iran Conflict
data.description: European countries are extending offers of assistance to Gulf nations as the conflict involving Iran persists. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing efforts by European governments to provide support in the region. The situation remains tense as the war continues, prompting international attention and diplomatic engagement. European nations are focusing on fostering stability and offering aid to mitigate the impact of the conflict on Gulf countries.

data.title: U.S. Refineries Increase Imports of Venezuelan Heavy Oil
data.description: American fuel manufacturers are increasingly purchasing Venezuela's heavy oil, which is anticipated to replace some of the barrels produced domestically. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the shift in sourcing as U.S. refineries adapt to changing market conditions. The move comes amid evolving geopolitical dynamics and economic considerations, as Venezuelan oil becomes more accessible. This trend reflects broader changes in the global oil market, influenced by supply chain adjustments and international relations. The increased importation of Venezuelan oil is expected to impact the U.S. energy sector, potentially altering production strategies and market shares.

data.title: Retailers Address Demand for 1990s Fashion Inspired by Carolyn Bessette Kennedy
data.description: Shoppers seeking to emulate Carolyn Bessette Kennedy's iconic 1990s style, reminiscent of her appearance in 'Love Story,' are finding it challenging to do so with Calvin Klein's current collections. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that other retailers are stepping in to meet this demand. The resurgence of interest in 1990s fashion has prompted various brands to offer alternatives that capture the essence of Bessette Kennedy's refined aesthetic. This trend underscores a broader movement in the fashion industry, where nostalgia-driven styles are gaining popularity among consumers.

data.title: Healthcare's Popularity in the Job Market Faces Uncertainty
data.description: Healthcare has become the leading career choice in the current job market. Bloomberg posted on X, raising concerns about the future when these job opportunities begin to diminish. The sector's appeal is driven by its stability and growth potential, but questions arise about the sustainability of this trend as the market evolves. The industry must adapt to changing demands to maintain its attractiveness to job seekers.

data.title: Brazil Central Bank Governor: Uncertain Impact of Middle East Conflict
data.description: The Governor of Brazil's Central Bank has expressed concerns over the uncertain consequences of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. According to Jin10, the governor highlighted that the geopolitical tensions could have unpredictable effects on global markets and economies. The situation remains fluid, and the potential ramifications are yet to be fully understood. The governor emphasized the importance of monitoring developments closely as the situation evolves.

data.title: WHO Confirms 13 Attacks on Healthcare Facilities in Iran
data.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has verified 13 incidents of attacks on healthcare facilities within Iran. According to Jin10, these assaults have raised significant concerns about the safety and security of medical infrastructure in the region. The WHO emphasizes the importance of protecting healthcare services, especially in conflict zones, to ensure uninterrupted access to medical care for affected populations. The organization continues to monitor the situation closely and urges all parties to respect international humanitarian laws that safeguard healthcare facilities and workers.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum options with a notional value of $2.68 billion will expire and settle on March 6.
data.description: Greeks.live published an article on the X platform stating that options settlement data on March 6th showed 32,000 Bitcoin options expiring, with a put-call ratio of 1.69, a maximum price threshold of $69,000, and a notional value of $2.3 billion; 184,000 Ethereum options expiring, with a put-call ratio of 0.85, a maximum price threshold of $1,950, and a notional value of $380 million. The crypto market rebounded this week, with Bitcoin stabilizing above the $70,000 mark and poised to challenge $75,000. However, options market data shows that selling call options has become the mainstream trading activity in the past two days, and although prices are still rising, the momentum has slowed. Tomorrow, 7% of total open interest in options will expire, almost at a recent low, while Bitcoin's open interest percentage has reached a recent peak. Benefiting from the recent rebound, the implied volatility of Bitcoin and Ethereum has increased this week, with BTC's primary term IV at 55% and ETH's primary term IV at 75%.

data.title: CoinShares: Institutions Maintain Stable Positions Amid 23% Bitcoin Pullback
data.description: CoinShares reports that despite a roughly 23% drop in Bitcoin's price from its all-time high of nearly $125,000 in early October, institutional investors have maintained a stable holding strategy. CoinShares analyst Matt Kimmell stated that professional investors have only slightly reduced their holdings, while long-term investors such as pension funds and sovereign wealth funds have continued to "quietly increase" their positions. The report notes that global Bitcoin ETF inflows remain positive, indicating that the fourth-quarter sell-off was more driven by profit-taking by long-term holders than by the withdrawal of new institutional funds. (Coindesk)

data.title: TD Securities: Unless non-farm payrolls are significantly weak, the US dollar will remain strong.
data.description: Strategists at TD Securities noted in a report that unless Friday's US non-farm payroll report is significantly weak, it is unlikely to have a major impact on the dollar. They stated that US economic data may take a backseat, with market focus shifting to the Middle East conflict and its potential impact on the Federal Reserve's ability to cut interest rates this year. The strategists said, "You need to see a much worse report, and an increase in the unemployment rate, to get the market back on this week's non-farm payroll data and reverse recent price movements." They believe that given the US's energy independence and the reduced prospect of interest rate cuts, the dollar should remain strong if oil prices remain high. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysis: US productivity grew faster than expected in Q4 last year, and cost control helped cool inflation.
data.description: U.S. labor productivity rose more than expected in the fourth quarter of 2025, further demonstrating that businesses are striving to improve efficiency to control costs. Data released Thursday by the Bureau of Labor Statistics showed that productivity (non-farm output per hour) grew at an annualized rate of 2.8% in the fourth quarter, following an upward revision to 5.2% growth in the third quarter. The median forecast from economists surveyed was 1.9%. This recent trend in productivity helps ensure that wage pressures are under control and confirms the view of Federal Reserve officials that the labor market is no longer a source of inflation. Labor costs are the largest expense for many businesses, so companies are turning to new technologies and equipment to improve employee efficiency. Business investment in technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI) has enabled some companies to operate with leaner workforces, contributing to last year's hiring slump. With AI investment continuing to rise, economists generally expect efficiency to continue to improve this year. (Jinshi)

data.title: UBS: A century of data proves that economic fundamentals are the core driver of the market.
data.description: UBS analysts noted in a report that data since 1900 shows that economic risk has proven more significant than geopolitical risk for financial markets. They stated that, in most cases, investors who can "see through" geopolitical noise perform best. The analysts said, "Through a simple regression analysis of future global stock returns and the geopolitical threat index, we found no correlation between the two, whether viewed from a monthly or yearly perspective." However, geopolitical risk is clearly crucial in the event of "extreme events with significant economic impact," citing World War I, World War II, and the 1973-1974 oil crisis as examples. But they pointed out that such events are "relatively rare," and historically, economic risk has always been more important to investors. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Defense Department Seeks Information on Key Mineral Reserves
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Defense is actively seeking information on reserves of five critical minerals as part of efforts to mitigate supply chain risks and bolster domestic raw material stocks. According to Jin10, the Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) issued a notice on Wednesday requesting details from potential suppliers regarding lithium, nickel, tin, chromium, and tellurium. The inquiry covers supplier intentions, product specifications, material sources, and market conditions.

The DLA, responsible for managing the National Defense Stockpile, aims to ensure the availability of metals essential for U.S. military needs. The agency is exploring potential procurement plans for 550 tons of lithium carbonate, 3,500 tons of nickel, 1,978 tons of London Metal Exchange-grade tin, 37 tons of tellurium, and 4,500 short tons of chromium. Additionally, the agency is consulting on the reprocessing or remelting of 1,978 tons of tin ingots affected by 'tin pest.' Based on LME prices, the cost of this tin batch is estimated to be close to $100 million.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Highlights AI Risks in U.S. Stock Market Impacting Dollar's Safe-Haven Status
data.description: Deutsche Bank has raised concerns about the influence of artificial intelligence risks in the U.S. stock market, suggesting it is undermining the dollar's position as a safe-haven currency. According to ChainCatcher, the bank's analysis indicates that the growing integration of AI technologies in financial markets is contributing to increased volatility and uncertainty, which in turn affects the dollar's reliability as a refuge during economic instability. This development comes amid broader discussions on the implications of AI advancements in various sectors, including finance, where its impact is being closely monitored by experts and institutions alike.

data.title: Strategists: Middle East Conflict May Influence Fed's Rate Cut Potential
data.description: Strategists from TD Securities have indicated that unless Friday's U.S. non-farm payroll report shows significant weakness, it is unlikely to impact the dollar substantially. According to Jin10, they suggest that U.S. economic data might take a backseat as market attention shifts to the Middle East conflict and its potential impact on the Federal Reserve's ability to cut rates this year. The strategists noted that a much weaker report and a rise in unemployment would be necessary to refocus market attention on this week's non-farm data and reverse recent price trends. They believe that given the U.S.'s energy independence and reduced prospects for rate cuts, the dollar should remain strong if oil prices stay elevated.

data.title: Avantis has opened applications for its second quarter airdrop.
data.description: Avantis, a leading decentralized derivatives exchange in the Base ecosystem, has opened applications for its second quarter airdrop. The airdrop will be distributed over four days, with 25% unlocked each day at 19:30 (Beijing time), starting today. The application period is 24 hours.

data.title: Iran claims it attacked US troops in Iraq
data.description: According to Iranian media outlet Fars News, Iran claims it used drones to attack US forces stationed in Iraq. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Barkin: Data temporarily puts aside the view that "we have beaten inflation".
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Barkin stated that the data has temporarily set aside the view that "we have beaten inflation," noting that we have seen relatively high inflation for several consecutive months. Textbooks would advise ignoring short-term shocks, but rising oil prices continue to significantly impact consumer confidence and spending. (Jinshi)

data.title: Nasdaq-listed YY Group announces the official launch of its Bitcoin Treasury strategy.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed YY Group announced the official launch of its Bitcoin Treasury strategy, making Bitcoin a primary reserve asset on its balance sheet to fulfill its commitments to capital preservation, balance sheet diversification, and long-term shareholder value creation. The company will reportedly allocate a portion of its excess cash reserves to Bitcoin and hold it long-term. (Prnewswire)
